questions.question: In synchronous digital circuits, what is the primary purpose of a 'reset' signal?
options: To temporarily halt the clock signal for debugging.
To force all sequential elements into a known, initial state.
To increase the operating frequency of the circuit.
To reduce the power consumption by deactivating idle blocks.
To prevent data hazards in pipelined architectures.

questions.question: In a multi-processor system with shared cache memory, what is the primary purpose of the MESI protocol?
options: To optimize the power consumption of cache blocks.
To ensure data consistency and coherence across multiple caches.
To define the instruction set architecture for the processors.
To manage virtual memory page mappings.
To accelerate the booting sequence of the system.

questions.question: During the physical verification phase of an integrated circuit, what is the main objective of Design Rule Checking (DRC)?
options: To verify that the implemented gates match the logical netlist.
To analyze the power consumption of the entire chip.
To ensure that the layout adheres to manufacturing process constraints and avoids fabrication errors.
To simulate the functional behavior of the circuit under various input conditions.
To determine the maximum operating frequency of the design.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of Dynamic Voltage and Frequency Scaling (DVFS) in modern processor and SoC designs?
options: To permanently increase the maximum performance of the device under all conditions.
To statically set the optimal voltage and frequency during manufacturing for maximum yield.
To dynamically adjust the operating voltage and frequency to balance performance and power consumption.
To prevent electromagnetic interference (EMI) between different functional blocks.
To implement fault tolerance by replicating critical processing units.

questions.question: In synchronous digital circuits, what is the primary purpose of the "hold time" requirement for a flip-flop?
options: To ensure that the data input remains stable for a minimum duration *after* the active clock edge.
To ensure that the data input arrives at least a minimum duration *before* the active clock edge.
To specify the maximum time allowed for the clock signal to propagate across the chip.
To define the minimum duration for which the clock signal must be high or low.
To control the power-up sequence of the flip-flop.
